purpose: Audit Waves 1-11 index refreshes individually, receipt/checkpoint writes, and note additions so current VBTPro build waves can copy the same evidence-output logic without over-refreshing.
source_authority_refs: git show --name-status for Wave 1-11 prep, implementation, refresh, closeout, and current protocol refresh commits

core_conclusion.wave_1_11_logic: Plan-note evidence moved every wave. Broad discovery indexes moved only when the selected wave or the next wave required fresh discovery, reconciliation, no-write auditing, scanner cleanup, closeout proof, or closeout artifact visibility.
core_conclusion.receipt_logic: Waves 1-7 and 9-10 did not write per-wave receipts. They used focused validators/tests plus active plan future-wave details. Receipts/checkpoints were written when the wave itself owned generated index outputs or at post-Wave-11 closeout.
core_conclusion.current_wave_match: The current build protocol now matches this by requiring every compact packet to carry a structured refresh_decision instead of relying on vague prose or default refreshes.

index_surface_matrix.wave_1_11_observed_logic: Rebuilt and committed as manifests/v2/plan_note_compiled.json whenever the active crack-repair plan or prep notes changed. This happened on every prep and implementation commit because future-wave details were persisted in the active plan.
index_surface_matrix.when_it_blocked_or_required_refresh: Required for plan/note durability after changing the plan; it did not imply a full repo-index refresh.
index_surface_matrix.current_wave_rule: Plan-note rebuild/status after roadmap, protocol, packet, or evidence-report changes; batch related plan-note edits and rebuild once.

index_surface_matrix.wave_1_11_observed_logic: Refreshed in Wave 8, pre-Wave 10, pre-Wave 11, post-Wave-11 closeout, and final closeout artifact visibility. Not refreshed after Waves 1-7 source/runtime implementations.
index_surface_matrix.when_it_blocked_or_required_refresh: Only when discovery/reconciliation was the wave, stale discovery blocked the next action, closeout required fresh proof, closeout artifacts needed discovery, or the operator asked.
index_surface_matrix.current_wave_rule: Use compact packet refresh_decision.repo_index_required; refresh after implementation commit only when future discovery or another durable condition requires it.

index_surface_matrix.wave_1_11_observed_logic: Projection refreshes appeared as part of full repo-index refresh/validation flows, not as independent wave proof. The committed Wave 1-11 refresh evidence centered on repo_index_summary, repo_index_hot, and repo_index_receipts; projection receipts were validation/discovery evidence.
index_surface_matrix.when_it_blocked_or_required_refresh: Required only when a full repo-index refresh or projection validation gate explicitly required projection freshness.
index_surface_matrix.current_wave_rule: Do not run projection refreshes for ordinary slice proof. Include them only when the selected refresh command or release/closeout gate explicitly requires projection freshness.

index_surface_matrix.wave_1_11_observed_logic: Refreshed when script/module discovery mattered: Wave 8 reconciliation, Wave 10/11 discovery prep, and closeout. New validators first proved waves by direct focused calls, not by immediate global index/allowlist routing.
index_surface_matrix.when_it_blocked_or_required_refresh: Required when future waves needed to discover validators/harnesses or when global routing in script_surface_usage_policy had to be reflected.
index_surface_matrix.current_wave_rule: Use compact packet refresh_decision.script_import_index_required; new validator existence alone is not enough.

index_surface_matrix.wave_1_11_observed_logic: Refreshed during Wave 8 and discovery/closeout refreshes when config topology changed or needed discovery; skipped in the final closeout artifact refresh because the closeout artifacts were not configs.
index_surface_matrix.when_it_blocked_or_required_refresh: Required when config-owned runtime or policy surfaces changed and later discovery/validation needed the inventory current.
index_surface_matrix.current_wave_rule: Use compact packet refresh_decision.config_variable_inventory_required; Wave 04A likely requires it after implementation because it creates config/schema/contract loader surfaces.

index_surface_matrix.wave_1_11_observed_logic: Refreshed with broad discovery/closeout gates and when new schema/receipt/checkpoint/report artifacts needed output-schema visibility.
index_surface_matrix.when_it_blocked_or_required_refresh: Required for generated output/schema discovery and closeout artifact indexing, not for ordinary focused runtime proof.
index_surface_matrix.current_wave_rule: Refresh only if a selected wave writes schema-bearing outputs whose discovery is needed by the next wave or closeout.

index_surface_matrix.wave_1_11_observed_logic: Wave 8 owned helper catalog reconciliation. Other waves did not update helper catalog just because they added validators.
index_surface_matrix.when_it_blocked_or_required_refresh: Required only when the helper catalog is the owner surface or when future helper discovery depends on it.
index_surface_matrix.current_wave_rule: Add validators to reference/repo_helpers/index.json only when they are reusable helper/control-plane surfaces, not simply because they are slice-owned.

per_wave_side_by_side.title: Activation schema, validator, and tests
per_wave_side_by_side.note_logic: Plan and compiled note updated during implementation; no separate prep was needed.
per_wave_side_by_side.receipt_logic: No per-wave receipt was written; proof lived in focused validator/test and active plan future-wave notes.

per_wave_side_by_side.title: Source registry live/historical acquisition semantics
per_wave_side_by_side.note_logic: Plan and compiled note updated during implementation; future-wave details captured acquisition semantics.
per_wave_side_by_side.receipt_logic: No per-wave receipt; no-network/live-source focused tests provided proof.

per_wave_side_by_side.note_logic: Prep note narrowed overlay taxonomy before implementation; implementation then appended future-wave overlay details.
per_wave_side_by_side.receipt_logic: No per-wave receipt; overlay validator and pytest were the proof.

per_wave_side_by_side.note_logic: Prep note captured bridge boundaries before adding the thin bridge.
per_wave_side_by_side.receipt_logic: No per-wave receipt; bridge validator and pytest were the proof.

per_wave_side_by_side.note_logic: Prep note captured status separation and production-readiness boundary.

per_wave_side_by_side.title: VBT642 mode-aware matrix and queue metadata
per_wave_side_by_side.note_logic: Two prep notes narrowed VBT642 mode semantics and future-wave details before implementation.
per_wave_side_by_side.receipt_logic: No per-wave receipt; matrix validator and pytest were the proof.

per_wave_side_by_side.title: Shared VBT tool boundary helper
per_wave_side_by_side.note_logic: Prep note defined shared-helper reuse after Waves 3-6 proved repeated semantics.
per_wave_side_by_side.receipt_logic: No per-wave receipt; shared-helper validator and pytest were the proof.
per_wave_side_by_side.implementation_subject: Add shared VBT tool boundary helper

per_wave_side_by_side.title: Helper manifest and script surface reconciliation
per_wave_side_by_side.note_logic: Prep note explicitly saved the refresh safety sequence and nonblocking pre-Wave-8 stale index evidence.
per_wave_side_by_side.receipt_logic: Index receipts/checkpoints were written because Wave 8 owned generated discovery reconciliation.
per_wave_side_by_side.implementation_subject: Reconcile Wave 8 helper and index discovery
associated_refresh_subjects: Reconcile Wave 8 helper and index discovery

per_wave_side_by_side.title: MCP/runtime precedence tests
per_wave_side_by_side.note_logic: Implementation updated plan notes with Wave 10 no-write follow-up requirements.
per_wave_side_by_side.receipt_logic: No per-wave receipt; the following refresh made Wave 9 discoverable for Wave 10 no-write audits.
per_wave_side_by_side.implementation_subject: Add Wave 9 MCP runtime precedence gate

per_wave_side_by_side.title: Read-only and index mutation cleanup
per_wave_side_by_side.note_logic: Prep note specified reusable no-write harness before implementation.
per_wave_side_by_side.receipt_logic: No per-wave receipt; the harness later scanned commands and the following refresh made it discoverable for Wave 11.

per_wave_side_by_side.title: Scanner, exact-path, and legacy authority cleanup
per_wave_side_by_side.note_logic: Prep notes added closeout plan/checklist; implementation updated plan with pass/fail semantics for scanner/index evidence.
per_wave_side_by_side.receipt_logic: Closeout receipt/checkpoint were written after all Waves 1-11 proof passed; final refresh indexed those closeout artifacts.
associated_refresh_subjects: Refresh indexes for post Wave 11 closeout

refresh_event_side_by_side_by_index.reason: Wave 8 explicitly owned helper manifest, script surface, repo-index, script-import, output-schema, config-inventory, and generated discovery reconciliation after Waves 1-7 created new owner surfaces.
not_refreshed_or_not_primary: plan_note_index was rebuilt because the active plan changed, but not the reason for the broad discovery refresh.
refresh_event_side_by_side_by_index.subject: Reconcile Wave 8 helper and index discovery

refresh_event_side_by_side_by_index.reason: Wave 9 added MCP/runtime precedence surfaces, and Wave 10 needed current discovery to audit no-write behavior around that new validator and related scripts.
not_refreshed_or_not_primary: helper catalog was not the owning surface in this refresh.
refresh_event_side_by_side_by_index.subject: Refresh Wave 10 discovery indexes

refresh_event_side_by_side_by_index.reason: Wave 10 added the read-only command harness and routed it in script_surface_usage_policy; Wave 11 needed that harness discoverable for scanner and exact-path cleanup.
not_refreshed_or_not_primary: plan-note status was checked, but the refresh target was discovery indexes.
refresh_event_side_by_side_by_index.subject: Refresh Wave 11 discovery indexes

refresh_event_side_by_side_by_index.reason: The closeout checklist required output-schema/full repo-index, script-import, and config-variable refreshes before the final Waves 1-11 validation bundle and status checks.
not_refreshed_or_not_primary: The refresh did not create the closeout receipt yet; that came in the following closeout commit.
refresh_event_side_by_side_by_index.subject: Refresh indexes for post Wave 11 closeout

refresh_event_side_by_side_by_index.reason: The closeout commit added the checkpoint and receipt and marked the plan complete, so the final refresh indexed those closeout artifacts and confirmed routing stayed stable.
not_refreshed_or_not_primary: config_variable_inventory was not rewritten because the closeout artifacts were not config surfaces.
refresh_event_side_by_side_by_index.subject: Refresh indexes after closeout commit

refresh_event_side_by_side_by_index.reason: The implementation changed root rules, the durable protocol, Wave 04A packet, build-wave validator, and tests; future Wave 04A discovery depends on those rule and validator surfaces.
not_refreshed_or_not_primary: plan-note index was rebuilt in the implementation commit before this generated-index commit; config inventory did not change because no config files changed.
refresh_event_side_by_side_by_index.subject: Refresh discovery indexes for refresh decision protocol

note_addition_logic.pattern: The active crack-repair plan was the running ledger. Prep commits and implementation commits both updated plans/v7_2_4/vbtprobuild_crack_repair_execution_plan_20260513.json plus manifests/v2/plan_note_compiled.json.
note_addition_logic.why_this_worked: Notes were compact, wave-specific, and future-facing. They captured order, owner files, validators, safety boundaries, index requirements, and activation gates without restarting broad architecture planning.
note_addition_logic.current_copy_rule: Each current VBTPro wave should update its packet or roadmap only when a discovery changes future implementation order, owners, validators, full-read requirements, safety boundaries, index requirements, or activation gates.

receipt_checkpoint_logic.why_this_worked: Focused validators/tests were enough for owner behavior proof. Receipts/checkpoints were reserved for generated index runs and the final closeout proof bundle, which avoided unnecessary committed artifact churn.
receipt_checkpoint_logic.current_copy_rule: Current waves should not create receipts just for status. Create receipts/checkpoints when the owner surface is a writer/generator/materializer or at a real closeout gate.

current_wave_alignment.logic_match: Matches Wave 8/Wave 10/Wave 11 conditional-refresh logic because Wave 04A is expected to create discoverable config, schema, contract, src, validator, and test surfaces needed by Wave 04B and helper/script discovery.
current_wave_alignment.anti_loop_guard: The packet and validator now reject vague refresh reasons, missing refresh decisions, skip timing when refresh surfaces are required, and next-wave discovery dependency without repo-index refresh.

other_index_surfaces_checked.wave_1_11_role: Not part of the crack-repair Wave 1-11 refresh chain; used as VBTPro lookup evidence in later VBT build planning.
other_index_surfaces_checked.current_rule: Use when VBTPro implementation requires native lookup; do not refresh it as part of generic wave closeout unless its owner task activates it.

other_index_surfaces_checked.wave_1_11_role: Wave 6 changed mode-aware VBT642 semantics, but this matrix was not refreshed as a generic discovery index in every wave.
other_index_surfaces_checked.current_rule: Use its owning validators when VBT642/export queue work is selected; do not treat it as a default closeout refresh surface.

other_index_surfaces_checked.wave_1_11_role: Covered in the main matrix because it was refreshed by Wave 8 and closeout discovery gates.
other_index_surfaces_checked.current_rule: Refresh only when output/schema artifact discovery is required by the selected wave or closeout gate.

evidence_output_chain_to_copy: Run required start and classify index/worktree noise.
Read active owner plan and selected packet, not unrelated historical notes.
If planning or prep changes are made, update JSON plan/packet/report and rebuild plan-note index once.
Implement owner surfaces and prove with owning validator and focused tests.
Run broad discovery index refresh only if the packet refresh_decision or closeout gate requires it.
Commit generated index outputs separately and stop; do not chase head-only staleness from the generated-index commit.

conclusion.main_guardrail: Every wave packet must name the owning validator/test and structured refresh_decision; index refreshes follow that decision instead of habit.
